title: Loki + Autonomi Ecosystem-Currency Plan
scope_principle: Track ONLY what loki-mode/autonomi CONSUMES from the Claude ecosystem (CLI invocation contract, auth detection, model IDs/pricing, stdout/exit-code parsing). Adjacent Anthropic PRODUCTS (Managed Agents, Claude Tag, loop-engineering primitives, agent identity) are peer/inspiration categories loki competes with, NOT dependencies -- assessed and declined with reason (the CTO triage move). Output is honestly small; no padding to a target count.

primary_sources_checked.claude_cli: claude --help / --version (installed 2.1.198): core flags loki consumes (-p, --output-format, --model, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --resume, --session-id, --permission-mode, claude auth status) ALL PRESENT and STABLE. No invocation-contract drift.
primary_sources_checked.model_overview: platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview (fetched 2026-07-01): GA models = claude-opus-4-8, claude-sonnet-5, claude-haiku-4-5(-20251001), claude-fable-5. Mythos 5 (claude-mythos-5) invitation-only via Glasswing, not GA.

stories.title: External-tool contract adapter + drift test (THE modular win)
stories.why: User mandate: 'if things we use change, we quickly adopt and release.' This session's keychain-login fix proves the need. Consolidate the claude/codex/cline/aider invocation + auth + model-ID contract behind one thin registry module with a DRIFT TEST that checks the installed CLI's ACTUAL contract vs loki's assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: A single module (extend providers/models.sh + a new providers/contract.sh) declares, per provider: the invocation flags loki relies on, the auth-status probe, and the model-ID source.

A drift test (tests/test-provider-contract-drift.sh) runs `claude --help`/`--version` (+ codex equivalents where installed) and FAILS if a flag loki invokes is absent, or a required auth probe is gone -- so a real CLI change is caught in CI, not by a user.
The test SKIPS gracefully (not fail) when a provider CLI is not installed (env-tolerant), and distinguishes 'flag missing (real drift)' from 'CLI absent (skip)'.
No behavior change to any existing loki command; drift test is additive.
Full gate: engine-adjacent, council 3/3 + local-ci; release as MINOR.

stories.title: Add Mythos 5 to the model catalog (opt-in, Glasswing-gated)
stories.why: claude-mythos-5 shipped (Fable 5 twin, $10/$50). It is invitation-only (Project Glasswing, defensive-cyber). Add as an OPT-IN catalog entry so approved users can select it; do NOT make it a default (most users lack access).
acceptance_criteria: providers/model_catalog.json gains a claude-mythos-5 entry with alias 'mythos', pricing $10/$50, note 'invitation-only via Project Glasswing; defensive-cyber; not GA'.
Mythos is NOT set as any latest_* default (Glasswing-gated); selectable only via explicit LOKI_CLAUDE_MODEL / alias.
loki provider models claude lists it with the availability note.
Dashboard model picker (if it enumerates the catalog) shows it flagged 'limited access'.

stories.title: Verify haiku pinned-ID + effort/adaptive-thinking currency
stories.why: Docs: haiku GA pinned ID is claude-haiku-4-5-20251001; effort defaults to high on Opus 4.8 + Sonnet 5; current models use adaptive (not extended) thinking. Confirm loki's alias resolution + effort/thinking handling match.
acceptance_criteria: Confirm loki's claude-haiku-4-5 alias resolves correctly (alias is documented-valid; no change if so).
Confirm loki's effort-per-tier handling does not FORCE a non-high effort that contradicts the new high default in a way that surprises users; document the interaction.
Confirm no loki code assumes extended-thinking on models that only support adaptive-thinking; note any mismatch.
Output: a short verification note; only code changes if a real mismatch is found (no speculative edits).

stories.title: Refresh doc/link references to platform.claude.com
stories.why: docs.anthropic.com now 301-redirects to platform.claude.com. Any loki docs/help/wiki that link to docs.anthropic.com model/pricing pages should point at the live host so users don't hit redirects.
acceptance_criteria: grep loki repo for docs.anthropic.com references in user-facing docs/help/wiki; update to platform.claude.com where the target moved.
Do NOT rewrite blog/news anthropic.com links that did not move; only the docs host.

assessed_not_applicable.reason: Hosted Anthropic PLATFORM. Loki-mode is a local CLI RARV orchestrator; it does not consume this API. Decoupled-brain/hands is a different product category -- inspiration only, not a currency obligation. A future 'loki on Managed Agents' would be a strategic build, founder-gated, not a staleness fix.

assessed_not_applicable.item: Claude Tag / multiplayer agents / agent identity
assessed_not_applicable.reason: Slack team-collaboration surface + per-channel identity model. Not loki-mode's surface. Loki's own dashboard-steering feature (task #44) is the analogous local capability we ARE building.

Claude Code HARNESS features. Loki-mode is a PEER loop-engine (RARV-C closure loop, completion council). It competes with / is inspired by these; it does not track them as a dependency. (We already use /goal + /loop in THIS session to drive loki dev, which is the correct consumer relationship -- as a Claude Code user, not as loki-the-product.)

Loki-mode already HAS an episodic/semantic memory system (memory/ package, consolidation.py) and cross-project RAG. Their approaches are useful inspiration for a future memory-consolidation enhancement (candidate backlog item), not a currency fix.

assessed_not_applicable.item: Enterprise-managed MCP auth (Okta/IdP), Claude apps gateway (Bedrock/GCP/Foundry)
assessed_not_applicable.reason: Enterprise identity + cloud-marketplace distribution. Relevant to autonomi-verify's enterprise RBAC/SSO story (top-100 rank 15, founder-gated), NOT a loki-mode CLI currency fix. Folded into the founder-gated prep (#48).

tracking: This file is the source of truth for EC-* stories. Update state (not-started -> in-progress -> done) as each ships. Companion human-readable summary: docs/ECOSYSTEM-CURRENCY-PLAN.md
